column6:
In Elf In Training, a chaotic 4-player couch co-op game, you play as Santa’s newest recruits delivering presents. The catch? You have to break into people’s homes to do it.

Homeowners, pets, and strange Christmas entities will defend their territory, so you’ll need to sneak, dodge, and outsmart them to complete your deliveries and prove you’re ready to become one of Santa’s elfs.
column13:
Hello! I’m an indie developer who has been creating small game projects for over 10 years. I studied game design and have also worked professionally in the game industry, with a background in QE and art.

My current project, Elf In Training, has been in development for about two years. While I’ve commissioned a few artists to help move the project along, about 90% of the work has been done by me. After more than a decade of working on smaller indie projects, I’m excited to finally be working toward my first commercial release.

I really appreciate MOHA and the artist community it supports. Thanks for having me!

column6: The Lost Shepherd is a cozy, narrative adventure game about escapism in the face of major world conflict. Journey through a shepherd’s dreams to gather sheep, discover lost artifacts, and find forgotten friends.
column12:
The Lost Shepherd is a cozy, narrative adventure game about escapism in the face of major world conflict. 
Journey through a shepherd’s dreams to gather sheep, discover lost artifacts, and find forgotten friends. Immerse yourself into surreal landscapes, puzzles, and stories to reconcile with a mysterious four-headed beast plagued by the shadows of a war-torn world.
column13:
Shear Luck Studios is a creative studio based in Austin, Texas, bringing to life our first indie game title, The Lost Shepherd – a cozy narrative game about sheep, war, and escapism.  

Founded by a dream team of six, we have multidisciplinary aspirations in animation, games, and collectible experiences. Our mission is to build entertaining worlds with charming characters and stories that explore the layers of complexity behind human conflict.

column6: A Rouge-like ttrpg Dungeon Crawler with the blood of Sudoku coursing through cursed veins. Brave the dungeon that shifts against you and pray you live to see the light of day.
column13: Making games and zines for freaks and fiends

column6:
Who Wants to Be Human? is a rogue-like match-3 where you build powerful relic combinations that turn simple swaps into massive chain reactions. Stack rule-breaking effects, create runaway synergies, and engineer explosive cascades to hit ever-increasing score targets.

Set in a twisted game show run by an unhinged demon, you play as townsfolk transformed into mice, competing to win back your humanity. The deeper you go, the more chaotic—and powerful—your runs become.

Design the system. Break the game. Let it spiral.
column13: I've worked in the games industry and in tech and now I'm doing full-time solo game dev. My youngest (13) is also pitching in with game design whilst my oldest (16) helps with testing.

column5: Jarrel Carr (Ja-rell) He/Him, Cristina Rosas (Cris-ti-na) She/Her, and Anai Arteaga (Ana-e) She/Her
column6: Come aboard to Mission Return, a space-survival game where players experiences the five hazards of Human Space Travel: radiation, isolation, distance from earth, gravity issues, and hostile environments. The players must manage these hazards to safely return back to home, to planet earth.
column11: Jarell Carr, Cristina Rosas, and Anai Arteaga
column13: We are Jarell, Cristina, and Anai. We are Communication Design majors at Texas State University.

column5: Caroline Trang ˈkærəlaɪn træŋ, she/her, Aylah Raumaker ("A-luh Raw-maker) she/her
column6: Three childhood friends have all gone their separate ways for college. While they go forward with their own personal lives, they still miss their childhood friendships. When one of the trio finally musters the courage to reach out, the friends must overcome their own challenges to learn how to make time for each other again, despite the distance.
column13:
We are Communication Design students at Texas State University taking Interactive III. 

My name is Aylah Raumaker, after I finish school I'm hoping to go into product design. I enjoy 3d modeling and UX/UI design. 

I'm Caroline Trang, a designer who loves adding a little bit of whimsy. I'll be graduating this year and going into UX/UI fields.

column5: Mykah Gonzalez, Alondra Acosta, Kristen Pham
column6: Mikah is a young boy whose once warm and loving family is torn apart by constant arguments, plunging him into quiet despair. Players step into his shoes to explore a distorted, dreamlike world shaped by his emotions. By searching for meaningful objects, fragments of happier memories, they can push back the darkness and uncover the truth behind the nightmare. Each discovery helps Mikah confront his inner fears and find a path toward healing.
column11: Mykah Gonzalez, Alondra Acosta, Kristen Pham
column13: We are senior students from Texas State University, majoring in UI/UX with a focus on user emotions and experiences. We share a passion for the horror genre and hope this game offers a thrilling experience while also conveying a deeply human message beneath the surface.

column6: ANAMOLY.OS is a narrative-driven investigation game where every decision carries weight. You play as a freelance Anomalous Entity Investigator, responding to emails from individuals experiencing strange and often dangerous phenomena—from cryptids to reality distortions. Using a digital anomaly guide, you must correctly identify each case and choose how to respond.

Gregory Aguirre - I am a senior at Texas State University and i am majoring in Communication Design, i am from Elgin,Texas and love sports and the brading aspect of design. I love learning more about design and challenging myself to explore different ideas in every aspect of my life.

column6: In this short cyberpunk mystery, step into the shoes of Sam Burnout as he investigates his grandmother’s violent disappearance. Navigate a filth-drenched world to gather clues and interrogate neighbors to uncover the truth. Developed by a dedicated duo, the game features a striking, unique art style and a hand-crafted narrative. Experience an intimate, atmospheric story where every conversation brings you closer to unraveling the mystery.
column13: Austin and Robby are communication design students at Texas State University, while they have not developed a game before Robby has extensive experience in world development and storytelling, while Austin has skills in developing and designing game features. Check both of us out on our websites to learn more!
